{"problem_standardized_math":"Consider the homotopy\n\\[\nH(x,\\lambda)=\\lambda(x^2-1)+(1-\\lambda)(x-a), \\qquad r(x)=x^2-1, \\qquad a=\\frac{1}{2}.\n\\]\nShow that, for this choice of \\(a\\), there exists a zero path of \\(H(x,\\lambda)=0\\) connecting \\(\\left(\\frac{1}{2},0\\right)\\) to \\((1,1)\\), and hence that continuation methods should work for this starting point."}
